name: Curried Vegetable Stew with Tamarind & Coconut Milk
recipeIngredient: 2 whole star anise, or dash of fennel
6 whole red chiles, Thai type, stemmed and thinly sliced crosswise
1 large red bell pepper, 1 inch thick slices
3 cups cauliflower florets, bite size pieces
4 ounces shiitake mushrooms or portobellos, 1/4 inch slices
1 1/2 cups garbanzo beans or yellow lentils
2 cups snow peas, or sugar snaps
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the tamarind in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Crush with a fork and set aside to soften for 1 to 2 hours, breaking up the pulp occasionally. Use a spoon or spatula to push through the pulp into a measuring cup. Discard the fibrous material.
2. Measure 1/3 cup of tamarind puree and set aside.
3. Combine the curry spices in a small bowl.
4. Heat oil over medium heat in a heavy 4-quart pot with a tight-fitting lid. When hot, add onion, ginger, garlic, and chiles; cook, stirring until onion is softened (about 5 minutes).
5. Add spices and cook, stirring until fragrant (about 3 minutes). Add red bell pepper, cauliflower, and mushrooms, sprinkling with salt. Stir, then continue cooking for 5 minutes.
6. Increase heat to high, add tamarind puree, coconut milk, and water; bring to a simmer. Reduce heat and gently simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Add garbanzo beans and simmer for 10 minutes until vegetables are tender. Add peas and simmer for 5 minutes until they are tender.
8. Remove star anise. Season with salt to taste and sprinkle with cilantro before serving.
